pending.body: Fix same-timestamp retry ordering in `latest_task_runs_per_bead`. In `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:379-389`, `compare_task_run_recency` treats a terminal row as newer than a later retry whenever both attempts share the same `started_at`, because it compares `finished_at` before anything else. `reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage` in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:163-204` uses that result to derive `active_bead` and progress, so a retry that starts at the same timestamp as an earlier failed/skipped attempt can leave the milestone in `ready` with `in_progress_beads=0` even though a new run is active. This needs a deterministic tie-break that preserves the newest attempt when timestamps collide, plus a regression test covering same-timestamp retries affecting snapshot state.

Add a recovery path for start retries when `run_id` is absent. `record_bead_start` writes lineage/snapshot before it appends the `BeadStarted` journal event (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:428-451`). If that final append fails, the retry logic only reuses the existing running row when a runless caller supplies the exact original `started_at` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:435-442`); otherwise `record_task_run_start` falls through to the "already active" error (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2815-2858`). For legacy callers still passing `run_id=None`, a transient journal failure leaves the start operation unrecoverable by a normal retry. The start matching/backfill logic should be widened so runless callers can repair the partial write, and this needs a regression test.

run_snapshot.status_summary: running: final review restart round 14 -> Plan and Implement
